meta.defaultDescription: A small apartment complex in Naupaktos, Greece, by the Venetian harbour and a few steps from the sea, the old town and the beach. Every apartment in one building.

home.heroSubtitle: A small apartment complex in the heart of Naupaktos, a few steps from the sea, the old town and the beach.
home.featuredSubtitle: Every apartment in the complex, each with its own character.
home.why1Text: The whole complex is a short walk from the harbour and the beach.
home.why2Text: Cared for by people who live here and answer fast.
home.why3Text: Check the calendar, send a request, get a personal reply.
home.why4Text: Clean, well-equipped spaces with everything you need.
home.locationTitle: A town between mountain and sea
home.locationText: Naupaktos wraps a tiny Venetian port in stone walls, with a fortress above and beaches on either side. Tavernas, cafes and history are all within walking distance.
home.contactCtaText: Send us a message and we will reply personally, as soon as we can.

about.title: Naupaktos, the town by the sea
about.intro: Naupaktos (Nafpaktos) sits on the north shore of the Gulf of Corinth, where the mountains of Nafpaktia meet the water. Its Venetian harbour, ringed by castle walls, is one of the most beautiful in Greece.
about.thing1Text: Start at the tiny Venetian port: walk the walls, have coffee on the water and find the statue of Cervantes.
about.thing2Text: Climb through the plane trees up to the fortress. The view over the gulf and the bridge is worth every step.
about.thing3Text: Psani and Gribovo are minutes on foot from the centre. For quieter water, Monastiraki and Chiliadou are a short drive.
about.thing4Text: Tavernas around the harbour serve fresh fish, mezedes and local wine. After dark, wander the old town lanes.
about.thing5Text: Messolonghi and its lagoon, the mountain villages of Nafpaktia (Ano Chora), or a day at Delphi.
about.thing6Text: End the day at the harbour lighthouse: the sun drops behind the Rio-Antirrio bridge.
about.gettingHereText: Naupaktos is an easy drive, minutes from the Rio-Antirrio bridge.

reviews.title: What our guests say
reviews.q1Text: “Morning coffee on the balcony looking at the gulf. The apartment was spotless and the harbour is five minutes on foot.”
reviews.q2Text: “The hosts answered instantly whenever we needed anything. A perfect base for the castle and the beaches.”
reviews.q3Text: “Quiet building, comfortable apartment, and Naupaktos won us over. We will be back.”

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: The things our guests usually ask before their stay.
faq.q1: What time are check-in and check-out?
faq.a1: Check-in from 15:00, check-out by 11:00. If you need different times, mention it in your booking request and we will do our best.
faq.a2: Yes, free parking at the complex for selected apartments. Check each apartment's amenities.
faq.q3: How does booking work? Do I need a card?
faq.a3: Pick your dates on the calendar and send a request. No card is needed and nothing is charged online. We confirm availability and the details personally.
faq.a4: Yes. Until your booking is confirmed there is no commitment at all. To cancel after confirmation, contact us as soon as you can.
faq.a5: In selected apartments, yes. Mention your pet in the request so we suggest the right one.
faq.q6: Do you have equipment for babies and kids?
faq.a6: A cot and a high chair are available on request, free of charge.
faq.q7: Is smoking allowed? Are there quiet hours?
faq.a7: Smoking is not allowed indoors. You are welcome to smoke on the balconies and in the courtyard. Parties and events are not allowed, and please respect the quiet hours.
faq.q8: Is there Wi-Fi and air conditioning?
faq.a8: Yes, every apartment has free Wi-Fi and air conditioning. You will find the Wi-Fi password inside the apartment and in your arrival instructions.
faq.a9: Yes. Every apartment comes with fresh linens and towels and is fully cleaned before each arrival. For longer stays we change them on request.
faq.q10: We arrive late at night. Is that a problem?
faq.a10: Not at all. Tell us your arrival time and we will arrange the key handover, whatever time you get here.
faq.q11: What does the price include? Are there extra fees?
faq.a11: The nightly price includes linens, towels, cleaning, electricity, water and Wi-Fi. There are no hidden charges. The final amount is confirmed with your booking.
faq.q12: How many people does each apartment sleep?
faq.a12: Capacity and bedrooms are listed on each apartment. Mention your group size in the request. An extra bed or a cot is available by arrangement.
faq.q13: How far are you from the beach and the centre?
faq.a13: The complex is a few steps from the sea and within walking distance of the harbour and the old town. You will receive the exact address and directions with your booking confirmation.
faq.q14: Do the apartments have an equipped kitchen?
faq.a14: Yes. Every apartment has a kitchen with a fridge, hobs, a kettle, a coffee maker, cookware and tableware. Everything you need for breakfast and simple cooking.
faq.a15: We do not serve breakfast. Every apartment has its own kitchen though, and you will find bakeries and cafes a few minutes away on foot.
faq.q16: Is there a lift or access for guests with reduced mobility?
faq.a16: There is a step-free ground-floor apartment, and some apartments are reached by lift (check each apartment's amenities). For specific accessibility needs, ask us before booking so we can describe the spaces precisely.
faq.q17: Can I leave my luggage before check-in or after check-out?
faq.a17: Yes, by arrangement we store your luggage free of charge, depending on the day's schedule.
faq.q18: Why book directly through your site?
faq.a18: You talk directly with us, with no middleman fees, and arrival times and special requests are easy to arrange.
faq.q19: How do I get to Naupaktos?
faq.a19: By car, over the Rio-Antirrio bridge (about 2 hours from Athens), or by KTEL intercity bus to Naupaktos. More distances and ideas on The Area page.
faq.q20: Are there a supermarket, a pharmacy and an ATM nearby?
faq.a20: Yes, all of them are within walking distance, in the centre of Naupaktos.
